2606.27808.title: Learning Complementary Action Modeling from Automotive Maintenance Instructions
2606.27808.paper_summary: A minute lexical variation can reverse the procedural meaning of an instruction even when the rest of the sentence remains unchanged. In automotive maintenance instructions, this pattern often appears when an action phrase turns an instruction into its procedural counterpart. The entities, modifiers, and surrounding context remain largely invariant, while the action phrase determines the procedural relation. We define this task as Complementary Action Modeling (CAM). Given a maintenance instruction, the goal is to identify or generate its procedural counterpart by modifying the action phrase while preserving the remaining sentence context. This task focuses on three aspects: distinguishing complementarity from surface similarity, controlling generation at the action-phrase level, and evaluating relational correctness using retrieval, overlap-based, and human evaluation. Using a German automotive maintenance dataset, we examine these questions through candidate matching and controlled Seq2Seq generation. The results show that complementary maintenance instructions are best modeled as procedural associations grounded in subtle lexical cues. They should therefore not be treated as ordinary cases of sentence similarity or synonym-based paraphrasing.
2606.27808.paper_summary_zh: 一個微小的詞彙變化可以逆轉指令的程序意義，即使句子的其餘部分保持不變。在汽車維護指令中，當一個動作短語將指令轉變為其程序對應時，這種模式經常出現。實體、修飾語和周圍的上下文基本保持不變，而動作短語則決定了程序關係。我們將這項任務定義為補充動作建模（Complementary Action Modeling, CAM）。給定一個維護指令，目標是通過修改動作短語來識別或生成其程序對應，同時保留其餘句子上下文。這項任務專注於三個方面：區分補充性與表面相似性、控制動作短語層級的生成，以及使用檢索、重疊基礎和人類評估來評估關係正確性。利用德國汽車維護數據集，我們通過候選匹配和受控的Seq2Seq生成來探討這些問題。結果顯示，補充維護指令最好被建模為基於微妙詞彙線索的程序關聯。因此，它們不應被視為普通的句子相似性或基於同義詞的改寫案例。
2606.27808.author: Jiaqi Wu et.al.
2606.27808.authors: Jiaqi Wu, Bai Li, Jochen Hartmann, Martin Gaedke, Sander Stuijk
